published_prior_art.cite: LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 ÔÇö longitudinal g-formula sequential-regression formulation.

LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 ÔÇö first-order longitudinal g-formula remainder and sequential double robustness; the displayed direct-backward reparameterization is a model-specific algebraic variant.

repo_prior_art.cite: No other in-catalogue Stat kernel states a two-period transition-composed g-formula minimax frontier.

published_prior_art.cite: BonviniKennedyKeele2025 ÔÇö explicitly studies two-time-point g-formula HOIF estimators and reports minimax-optimal rates in some anisotropic/additive settings; its public abstract does not establish inclusion in either direction.
The finite-H├Âlder exact-smoothness construction is standard analysis; its role as a transition-channel separation is model-specific.
RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR applies HOIF methodology to time-dependent treatment but does not state a scalar-versus-multi-index transport-chain correction criterion.
RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR ÔÇö the single-time missing-response HOIF benchmark after removal of longitudinal composition.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:two-channel-minimax-frontier
soundness_flags.one_line: Its stated mixture lower bound is only \(\max\{n^{-r_1},n^{-r_2}\}\); when both \(r_j>1/2\), this is \(o(n^{-1/2})\), so it cannot imply the claimed necessary half of ÔÇťroot-n possible exactly whenÔÇŁ; the headline needs an explicit regular parametric-submodel lower bound \(\gtrsim n^{-1/2}\) and a combined lower bound.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:two-channel-minimax-frontier / def:direct-backward-estimator
soundness_flags.one_line: ÔÇťBounded Hellinger affinityÔÇŁ lacks the required direction (it must be bounded away from zero, or an equivalent TV/Hellinger control), and the estimator invokes an undefined block truncation precisely when \(K_j>n\) makes the displayed empirical Gram inverse singular.

soundness_flags.label: ass:outcome-state-sufficiency
soundness_flags.one_line: Exact removal of all earlier-history outcome dependence by a finite frozen \(H_2\) is load-bearing; a generic held-out residual-dependence check cannot validate the exact restriction, and no named protocol or design evidence establishes that it is realistic.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the lower claim by \(\mathfrak R_n\gtrsim\max\{n^{-1/2},n^{-r_1},n^{-r_2}\}\), explicitly give the regular parametric submodel, and restate the root-n iff only after that lower bound is included.

Define the blockwise projection construction for \(K_j>n\): block sizes, independent Gram estimates, aggregation, and the resulting variance/remainder bound; also replace ÔÇťbounded Hellinger affinityÔÇŁ with a uniform positive-affinity or TV bound.

Name a concrete euglycemic-clamp protocol and justify exact state sufficiency from its measurement/assignment design, or weaken the paper to an explicitly conditional theoretical model without empirical validation language.
Prove or redesign the normalized fuzzy perturbations so division by the rough propensity does not remove the advertised \(C^t\) transition or \(C^\beta\) outcome regularity in asymmetric regimes.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for a new, corrected headline: repair the root-n converse and non-root-n estimator definition before reopening novelty review.
